meta.title: Product Keynote: Astana International Finance Centre

transcript: Well good afternoon everyone. Before I start, some of you may know that on the other side of this city on the Al Maryah Island, Abu Dhabi Global Markets is hosting what's called Abu Dhabi Finance Week. And last year, one year ago, there on the balcony of Rosewood Hotel, first time I've met Akshay BD, some of you may know he is a non-CMO of Solana Foundation Institute. And pretty much it was a first time I've heard about Solana, I learned about Solana. Babak has introduced me to him. But since then, in a year, we've created a Solana Superteam. We've created a Solana Special Economic Zone. Lily was I believe twice in Kazakhstan and once she even moderated discussion with the President of our country. And this year in September, one of the fund managers in our jurisdiction have launched arguably the first Solana ETF with staking. Arguably I say because the one in the US came in October, so I think that the one launched in AIFC was the very first one. So what is this place that has been developing this digital industry and working with Solana so actively? It is called Astana International Finance Centre. Basically it's a special economic zone but also a jurisdiction based on a common law that is situated in the capital of Kazakhstan in the city of Astana. It's similar to Abu Dhabi Global Markets, to Dubai International Financial Centre, focused on capital market but we are also very crypto friendly. Today we rank 68 among the leading financial centers by the Global Financial Centers Index. We are number one in region of Central Asia and Eastern Europe. And it's a big... and we are the only ones, so we don't have any competition actually in the region. And now we are home for more than 4,800 companies who are registered with us. And I have to say that more than a half of them registered in last two years in 2024 and 25. But again, what makes us crypto friendly is our regulation. So just quick overview, it's actually quite a new jurisdiction and new regulation. From 2024, basically last year we have introduced so many new products or the regulations and rules for the products. And prior to that for one year we had a pilot project with our Central Bank actually building this bridge for DeFi between the commercial banks and the crypto exchanges that are licensed with AIFC. So Binance, Bybit, WhiteBIT, they have licenses with AIFC. And we have seven more our local exchanges. But now there are plenty of other types of licenses for digital asset service providers like crypto funds, crypto brokers and etc. And we see that this industry is growing quite fast. One of the key elements of our regulation is FinTech Lab. And you have to create this environment, controlled environment that will help to launch products safely. As you can see from the stats we have now 24 applicants in our FinTech Lab. You can see that in our regulation we generally have 29 different digital asset service providers. And then few stats again. As I said like 23 was a pilot year, the full working regulation was 2024 and all the participants, all the digital asset service providers volume exceeded 1.8 billion US dollars. And you can see this year already in nine months it's almost 7 billion. That's a growth of three times. So again, this is a very active growing market and it has I believe lot more potential. We also allow stablecoins to be issued, created. You can see some of the requirements for the those who want to apply for stablecoin licenses. The only thing it cannot be done in a local currency which is Kazakhstani Tenge. But otherwise we already have one license holder for stablecoin. And what I wanted to say is that our regulator itself accepts payments for its services and fees in stablecoins. So we recently have accepted first time with the facilitation of Bybit payments in stablecoin for the services or fees of the regulator which is called Astana Financial Services Authority. And last thing I want to brag about is that our regulation was just recently assessed by the IOSCO. For those who don't know it's an International Organization of Securities Commissioners. They have reviewed 20 jurisdictions who have actually in place regulation for digital assets. And AIFC was among top who hit all the requirements or recommendations that IOSCO made. And we've been in few times mentioned as the role model. Of course a lot of these things that we implemented still need be tested by time, tested by the participants. But again we are recognized as only four jurisdictions who have, who tag all the requirements that IOSCO made. Again thanks for your attention. I hope I have given just enough information to get you interested but left you with a lot more questions so you can find us, write us and learn a little bit more about us. Thank you.

sources.title: VanEck Debuts Solana ETF: VSOL Launches with Zero Fees
sources.snippet:
-

# VanEck Debuts Solana ETF: VSOL Launches with Zero Fees

Share

_VSOL sponsor fees will be waived for the first $1 billion in AUM from November 17, 2025 through February 17, 2026. Thereafter, the sponsor fee will be 0.30%. Brokerage fees and commissions may apply. Please check with your broker._

**_Investments in the_** [**_VanEck Bitcoin ETF (“HODL”)_**](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vaneck.com%2Fus%2Fen%2Finvestments%2Fbitcoin-etf-hodl%2Fhodl-prospectus.pdf&esheet=54358954&newsitemid=20251117326608&lan=en-US&anchor=VanEck+Bitcoin+ETF+%28%26%238220%3BHODL%26%238221%3B%29&index=1&md5=a1ac288b86eae8c1b2c7e4841a392e1d) **_, the_** [**_VanEck Solana ETF (“VSOL”)_**](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vaneck.com%2Fus%2Fen%2Finvestments%2Fsolana-etf-vsol%2Fvsol-prospectus.pdf&esheet=54358954&newsitemid=20251117326608&lan=en-US&anchor=VanEck+Solana+ETF+%28%26%238220%3BVSOL%26%238221%3B%29&index=2&md5=fc24029d3ccd5909f6991b0ad6485c73) **_, and the_** [**_VanEck Ethereum ETF (“ETHV”)_**](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vaneck.com%2Fus%2Fen%2Finvestments%2Fethereum-etf-ethv%2Fethv-prospectus.pdf&esheet=54358954&newsitemid=20251117326608&lan=en-US&anchor=VanEck+Ethereum+ETF+%28%26%238220%3BETHV%26%238221%3B%29&index=3&md5=11372d0c9d11a3d933cf8e06e11653fb) **_(collectively, the “Trusts”) are subject to significant risk and may not be suitable for all investors. The values of Bitcoin, Solana, and Ethereum are highly volatile, and you can lose your entire principal investment. The Trusts are not investment companies registered under the Investment Company Act of 1940 (the “1940 Act”) and therefore are not subject to the same protections as mutual funds or ETFs registered under the 1940 Act._**

NEW YORK--( [BUSINESS WIRE](https://www.businesswire.com))-- [VanEck](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vaneck.com%2Fus%2Fen%2F%3Futm_source%3Dpr%26utm_medium%3Dpressrelease&esheet=54358954&newsitemid=20251117326608&lan=en-US&anchor=VanEck&index=4&md5=de0d0ef9cac5fe95fb253faee1b528ee) is introducing the [VanEck Solana ETF (VSOL)](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vaneck.com%2Fus%2Fen%2Finvestments%2Fsolana-etf-vsol%2F&esheet=54358954&newsitemid=20251117326608&lan=en-US&anchor=VanEck+Solana+ETF+%28VSOL%29&index=5&md5=1ca2664152b054cacfa5fb8c3e29c91f), offering investors exposure to SOL, the native token of the Solana network, and to the staking rewards earned for helping secure the network (please see the disclosures below for information related to staking).

> “We’re excited to be launching VSOL and to build on VanEck’s long history of expanding access to digital assets through thoughtful, investor-focused products,” Kyle DaCruz, Director, Digital Assets Product with VanEck.
>
> Share

VanEck is waiving its sponsor fee for VSOL at launch for the first $1 billion in assets or until February 17, 2026, whichever comes first. During this same period, VSOL’s third-party staking service provider will also waive its fee for staking services. If VSOL’s assets exceed $1 billion before February 17, 2026, a 0.30% sponsor fee will apply to assets above that threshold. After February 17, 2026, the sponsor fee will be 0.30%.

Solana’s historical high-performance, low-cost architecture has made it a leading blockchain for onchain activity, processing tens of millions of transactions per day across decentralized finance (DeFi), gaming, NFTs, and tokenized real-world assets. Built for scalability and efficiency, Solana combines a Proof of History (PoH) mechanism with Proof of Stake (PoS) consensus, enabling fast block times and low transaction costs. Validators stake SOL to verify transactions, secure the network, and earn staking rewards in return.

sources.title: Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) Surpasses $500M in Assets Under Management
sources.snippet:
Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) Surpasses $500M in AUM
# Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) Surpasses $500M in Assets Under Management
San Francisco •Nov 21, 2025
![Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) Surpasses $500M in Assets Under Management](https://bitwiseinvestments.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.datocms-assets.com%2F62087%2F1762788442-bsol-bitwise-solana-staking-etf-press-500m-aum.png&w=3840&q=75)
Bitwise Asset Management, the global crypto asset manager with over $15 billion in client assets, announced today that the Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) has surpassed $500 million in assets under management (AUM), achieving the landmark figure in its first 18 days of trading.
"We are truly in awe of the speed and scale of BSOL’s growth," said Bitwise CEO Hunter Horsley. "Crypto is becoming a mainstream asset class. BSOL hitting half a billion dollars in AUM this quickly is a clear testament to investors’ belief in the Solana ecosystem as a cornerstone of the future of the crypto industry."
As one of the first crypto ETPs with built-in staking, BSOL aims to stake 100% of the Fund’s SOL holdings through Bitwise Onchain Solutions, powered by Helius, the leading Solana staking technology known for its high performance, technical excellence, and security. The strategy seeks to maximize investors’ participation in Solana’s average staking rewards of over 7%.¹
BSOL has a net sponsor fee of 0.00% for the first three months on its first $1 billion in assets. All staking fees are also waived for the first three months. After three months, the sponsor fee will be 0.20% and the staking fee, which applies only to the staking rewards that BSOL earns, will be 0.06%.
###### **Note:**
###### (1) Staking reward rate is based on the annualized 90-day average as of 11/20/25, with data from Helius. Rewards are subject to change and should not be considered an indication of the Fund’s performance. The tax treatment of rewards earned by the Trust may have potentially adverse consequences for shareholders. Please consult with your tax advisor.
### Risks and Important Information
**This material must be accompanied by a prospectus. Please read the prospectus carefully before investing. To obtain a current prospectus visit**[**bsoletf.com/welcome**](https://bsoletf.com/welcome)**.**
**The Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) is not suitable for all investors. An investment in BSOL is subject to a high degree of risk, has the potential for significant volatility, and could result in significant or complete loss of investment. BSOL is not an investment company registered under the Investment Company Act of 1940, as amended (the “1940 Act”) and therefore is not subject to the same protections as ETFs and mutual funds registered under the 1940 Act. An investment in BSOL is not the same as a direct investment in Solana (SOL).**
Shares of ETPs are bought and sold at market price (not NAV) and are not individually redeemed from the Fund. Brokerage commissions will reduce returns. The NAV may not always correspond to the market price of SOL and, as a result, Creation Units may be created or redeemed at a value that is different from the market price of the Shares. Authorized Participants’ buying and selling activity associated with the creation and redemption of Creation Units may adversely affect an investment in the Shares.
The amount of SOL represented by a Share will continue to be reduced during the life of the Fund due to the transfer of the Fund’s SOL to pay for the Sponsor’s management fee, and to pay for litigation expenses or other extraordinary expenses. This dynamic will occur irrespective of whether the trading price of the Shares rises or falls in response to changes in the price of SOL.
There is no guarantee or assurance that the Fund’s methodology will result in the Fund achieving positive investment returns or outperforming other investment products.
Investors may choose to use the Fund as a means of investing

# **Franklin Templeton Expands Its Digital Asset ETP Suite With Launch of Franklin Solana ETF (SOEZ)**
Share
*SOEZ provides institutional access to one of the fastest growing blockchain networks through a regulated, transparent ETF structure*
SAN MATEO, Calif.--([BUSINESS WIRE](https://www.businesswire.com))--Franklin Templeton, a global investment leader with more than $1.6 trillion in assets under management, today announced the launch of Franklin Solana ETF (NYSE Arca: SOEZ) (the “Fund”), an exchange traded product (ETP) designed to provide investors with exposure to the price movements of Solana (SOL), the native token of one of the most active and scalable blockchain networks. SOEZ seeks to reflect generally the performance of the price of Solana and the rewards from staking as much of the Fund’s Solana as is practicable (up to 100%), before payment of the Fund’s expenses and liabilities. The Fund receives staking rewards in the form of Solana tokens (SOL), which may be treated as income. Staking Solana on the network may also enhance network security and stability. The Fund’s SOL cash value is calculated by valuing the SOL held by the Fund based on the CME CF Solana-Dollar Reference Rate, New York

editorial.headline: Kazakhstan Beats Wall Street to Launch First Staking Solana ETF
editorial.standfirst: While US regulators stalled on staking rewards, the Astana International Finance Centre (AIFC) executed a world-first listing in September. The move cements Kazakhstan’s pivot from a resource economy to a nimble, common-law crypto jurisdiction.
key_takeaways: Fonte Capital launched the Fonte Solana ETF (SETF) on AIX in September 2025, beating US issuers.
AIFC digital asset volume surged 3x year-over-year to nearly $7B in the first nine months of 2025.
The AIFC regulator now accepts licensing fees in stablecoins, facilitated by Bybit.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
While the US Securities and Exchange Commission spent late 2025 forcing asset managers to amend filings, the **Astana International Finance Centre (AIFC)** quietly delivered what Wall Street could not. In September 2025, **Fonte Capital Ltd.** listed the **Fonte Solana ETF (SETF)** on the Astana International Exchange (AIX). Unlike its eventual American counterparts, this product launched with staking rewards fully integrated from day one, effectively capturing the "holy grail" of institutional crypto exposure months before VanEck or Bitwise could clear regulatory hurdles.

This speed is by design, not accident. Governor **Renat Bekturov** positioned the AIFC as a "regulatory sandbox with teeth"—a common law jurisdiction nestled in Central Asia that prioritizes execution over bureaucracy. The results are quantifiable: digital asset volume in the zone hit nearly **$7 billion** in the first nine months of 2025, a threefold increase from the previous year. This growth is underpinned by the creation of a dedicated **Solana Economic Zone**, a strategic initiative born from a meeting with the Solana Foundation's **Akshay BD** just twelve months prior.

Perhaps the most bearish signal for legacy finance—and the most bullish for onchain utility—is the regulator's own operational shift. The AIFC has begun accepting licensing fees in stablecoins, a payment rail facilitated by **Bybit**. When a sovereign-backed regulator moves from regulating crypto to *transacting* in it, the window for regulatory arbitrage narrows significantly. For institutional allocators, the AIFC has graduated from a frontier curiosity to a viable, high-speed alternative to the sluggish regulatory machinery of the West.
